name: Calzone
recipeInstructions:
-------------------------------SPINACH FILLING-------------------------------
10 1/2 ounces Frozen chopped spinach
Garlic powder
2 tablespoons Olive oil
1 cup Provolone cheese, grated
1 cup Mozzarella cheese, grated
Remove dough from machine and roll it into circles of approximately 8 inches. Spread filling on one half of the circle leaving a border around it for closing.
Close the calzone by folding the unfilled side over the filled side and crimping the edges closed with your fingers or a fork. Place on a lightly greased baking pan. Let rise approximately 30 minutes. Brush lightly with olive oil and bake at 500°F (260°C) for 20-30 minutes, or until puffed and golden.
The original recipe says to put 1 1/2 to 2 tablespoons each of ricotta and grated mozzarella cheese in each calzone.
My favorite filling is the following:
SPINACH FILLING: Saute thawed spinach in olive oil and garlic powder until all water has evaporated. (I squeeze out excess liquid first.) Mix cheeses into spinach. You may substitute frozen chopped broccoli for spinach.
I LOVE these! I serve them with a spaghetti sauce that has sausage, peppers, onions, mushrooms, etc., and they are GREAT! Hope you enjoy them! I used to eat them at the mall and wish I knew how to make them! Let us know what you think!
This recipe is found in Donna German's book II.
